diff_summary: Pinned commit 20b86ef touches only the PULL side (wc-sync-pull per_page); the stock-PUSH story under review lives in the working tree: packages/woocommerce/src/{stock-push,sync,reconciliation,client}.ts, packages/contracts/src/woocommerce.ts, packages/catalog/src/stock-adjustments.ts, apps/api/src/routes/pos/sales.ts, apps/jobs/src/jobs/{wc-outbox-drain,wc-stock-reconcile}.ts. enqueueStockPush coalesces per-product mutations into one debounced wc_outbox row (latest value wins) keyed by wc-stock:{productId}; the outbox drain PUTs an ABSOLUTE stock_quantity to Woo and marks the row done; a nightly job reconciles drift.
ac_coverage: AC1 (all mutation paths enqueue: POS sale + adjustStock for goods-received/stock-take/manual; online fulfilment intentionally not re-pushed) implemented; AC2 (SKU/woo_product_id mapping, unmapped = no-op) implemented + tested; AC3 (stock_quantity + stock_status via stockStatusFor, PUT) implemented + tested; AC4 (per-SKU debounce/coalesce to final value) implemented + tested. AC5 (SKU mapping admin screen + bulk CSV) and AC6 (nightly drift report) implemented (sku-mapping.ts, reconciliation.ts, wc-stock-reconcile.ts). GAP: no test covers the drain-vs-re-enqueue ordering/last-write-wins race, which the implementation does not actually guard against (see findings).

findings.title: Lost update: drain unconditionally marks a row done, clobbering a concurrently re-armed newer stock value
findings.detail: enqueueStockPush coalesces by re-arming the SAME wc_outbox row (onConflictDoUpdate on idempotency_key wc-stock:{productId}, setting request=newValue, status='pending', nextAttemptAt=now+debounce, doneAt=null). The drain (apps/jobs/src/jobs/wc-outbox-drain.ts) claims due rows with claimDueWcWritebacks, which is a plain SELECT that does NOT transition the row out of 'pending' or lock it. The drain then awaits the Woo PUT (a network round-trip), and on success calls markWcWritebackDone, which runs UPDATE wc_outbox SET status='done' WHERE id=? UNCONDITIONALLY (sync.ts:230-238) -- no guard on the row's value/version/claimed-state. Race window: drain claims V1, begins PUT; meanwhile the API process (POS sale settleSale() at sales.ts:123, or adjustStock at stock-adjustments.ts:79) calls enqueueStockPush and overwrites that exact row with the newer value V2 (status back to 'pending', doneAt=null); the PUT returns; markWcWritebackDone forces the just-re-armed V2 row to 'done'. V2 is now silently dropped -- never drained, never dead-lettered, no retry. Because updateProductStock PUTs an ABSOLUTE stock_quantity (client.ts:269-280), Woo is left at V1. When V1>V2 (e.g. restock-to-10 in flight, then a sale drops local to 2), Woo shows MORE stock than reality -> overselling online, the exact failure this story exists to prevent. This is the API process and the jobs process running genuinely concurrently, so the in-process scheduler overlap guard (runner.ts) does not help. Violates the story's core invariant: 'a stale stock value can't overwrite a newer one' and 'a failed push is retried via the outbox without losing the update'. The nightly reconciliation (AC6) would only REPORT the resulting drift; it never repairs it.
findings.fix: Make markWcWritebackDone a conditional, optimistic write that cannot clobber a row that was re-armed after it was claimed. Capture the snapshot the drain pushed (its request/updated state) at claim time and only transition to 'done' when the row is unchanged. Concretely: (a) add a monotonically increasing version/revision column to wc_outbox bumped on every enqueueStockPush re-arm, claim it into the drain's row snapshot, and mark done with `UPDATE ... SET status='done' WHERE id=? AND version=?`; OR (b) gate done on the request still matching: `UPDATE ... SET status='done', doneAt=now, lastError=null WHERE id=? AND status='pending' AND request=:pushedRequest` -- if 0 rows update, a newer mutation re-armed the row, so leave it pending (it will be drained again with the final value). Either way, claimDueWcWritebacks should also transition claimed rows to an in-flight state (e.g. status='in_flight' with a claimed_at) under FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED so a re-enqueue distinguishes 'mutate the in-flight snapshot' from 'arm a fresh pending push'. Add a regression test: claim a row, re-arm it with a newer value mid-flight, complete the push, assert the row is still pending and carries the newer value (not done).

findings.title: No test asserts push ordering / final-value-wins across the enqueue+drain boundary
findings.detail: AC4's coalescing is tested only at the enqueue layer (stock-push.test.ts collapses N mutations to one row with the final value). There is no integration test exercising enqueue -> drain -> re-enqueue-mid-drain, which is exactly where the lost-update race above hides. The story's stated review focus ('idempotent + ordered, a stale value can't overwrite a newer one') is therefore unverified end-to-end. Even if the maintainer disputes the severity of the race window, the absence of any ordering test means a future refactor of claim/done could silently regress the guarantee.
findings.fix: Add an integration test across the enqueue/drain boundary that interleaves a re-enqueue between claimDueWcWritebacks and markWcWritebackDone (e.g. via an instrumented client whose updateProductStock callback re-arms the row before resolving) and asserts: the row remains pending with the newer value, the drain does not strand the update, and the final Woo push carries the latest stock. Pairs with the markWcWritebackDone guard fix.

findings.title: No DB-level CHECK guaranteeing stock_qty >= 0 (relies entirely on application clamps)
findings.detail: products.stock_qty has no non-negative CHECK constraint (schema/products.ts:30). Local negative stock is prevented only in application code: the POS decrement is guarded by WHERE stock_qty >= qty (sales.ts:120) and adjustStock clamps via Math.max(0, next) (stock-adjustments.ts:56). These cover the paths in this story, so there is no live negative-stock bug here. But any future writer that issues a raw decrement without the guard could drive it negative, and stockStatusFor already defensively treats <=0 as outofstock -- a defense that only matters if the invariant can be broken. This is hardening, pre-existing to the schema, and out of this story's scope.
findings.fix: Defer. Optionally add a CHECK (stock_qty >= 0) in an additive migration as belt-and-suspenders for the source-of-truth invariant; not required by this story.
